Title
Hit Detection and Hurtboxes Prompt

Purpose
Upgrade Shadow Shift's combat collision into a clearer hitbox/hurtbox system with stronger timing control and cleaner extensibility for enemies, bosses, and future ranged attacks.

Why This Matters for V2
Combat quality depends on trust. If the player cannot tell what can hit, when it can hit, and why a hit happened, the game feels unfair even when values are technically correct.

Detailed Codex Instructions
Context:
The current game already supports melee combat and enemy contact damage. V2 needs a more intentional hit detection layer without replacing the entire architecture.

Goal:
Implement a clearer hurtbox/hitbox model with better lifetime control, ownership, filtering, and debug visibility.

Implementation Steps:
1. Audit current melee collision, enemy damage checks, hazard damage, and any projectile or special attack overlaps.
2. Separate hurtboxes from hitboxes conceptually and in code.
3. Add structured hitbox definitions with fields like:
   - owner id/type
   - team/faction
   - lifetime
   - damage
   - knockback
   - hit tags
   - elemental tags
4. Add hurtbox filtering rules so allies, invulnerable states, or already-hit targets are handled cleanly.
5. Introduce optional debug rendering toggles for hitboxes and hurtboxes.
6. Preserve the current simple geometry approach.

Constraints:
- No heavy physics engine
- Keep performance reasonable
- Preserve current combat feel while improving correctness

Implementation Notes
- One clean combat overlap pipeline is better than many ad hoc checks.
- Build with future bosses, ranged enemies, and environmental attacks in mind.
- Make repeat-hit prevention explicit rather than incidental.

Validation Checklist
- Melee still lands reliably
- Enemy contact or attack damage still works
- Hit ownership and filtering are clearer
- Debugging combat collisions is easier
- The system is ready for richer attack types
